findings.evidence: Line 5 is `public import Mathlib.Logic.Equiv.Fintype`; the only grep hits for `exists_extending_pair` in this module are the doc/comment and private proof at lines 77-82, while public theorem statements below do not mention it.
findings.fix: Change this to a non-public implementation import, or make the permutation-extension wrapper part of the intended public API if the roadmap milestone is being delivered here.
findings.issue: `Mathlib.Logic.Equiv.Fintype` is publicly imported only to prove the private helper using `Equiv.Perm.exists_extending_pair`, so importing `Contractability` unnecessarily exposes that dependency downstream.

summary: The theorem surface is mostly on target, but the module publicly re-exports a proof-only Mathlib dependency. Keep implementation-only imports out of the public interface unless their declarations appear in exported signatures.
